For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Fully Funded PhD Position in Just and Inclusive Urban Mobility Transition at Delft University of Technology (TU Delft), Netherlands 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice 2027–2028: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Health Policy Leaders
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Commonwealth Fund has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2027–2028 Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice, an exceptional international fellowship program designed for mid-career professionals committed to transforming health care systems and improving health outcomes. The Harkness Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for emerging and established health policy leaders to conduct innovative […]

The post Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice 2027–2028: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Health Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Commonwealth Fund has officially opened applications for the prestigious 2027–2028 Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice, an exceptional international fellowship program designed for mid-career professionals committed to transforming health care systems and improving health outcomes.

The Harkness Fellowship offers a unique opportunity for emerging and established health policy leaders to conduct innovative research, engage with leading experts, and contribute to policy reforms that improve health care access, quality, efficiency, and equity. Through this highly competitive fellowship, participants undertake a policy-oriented research project with relevance to both the United States and their home country while expanding their professional networks and leadership capacity.

Applications for the 2027–2028 cohort are open from June 1, 2026, to November 1, 2026.

About the Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowship

The Harkness Fellowship is one of the world’s most respected international health policy fellowships. It is administered by The Commonwealth Fund, a private foundation dedicated to promoting a high-performing health care system that achieves better access, improved quality, and greater efficiency, particularly for society’s most vulnerable populations.

The fellowship is specifically designed for professionals who have demonstrated excellence in health policy and practice and who have the potential to become influential leaders in shaping the future of health care systems.

Fellows spend time developing and implementing a research project that addresses pressing health policy challenges and contributes to meaningful policy or system-level change.

Fellowship Objectives

The program focuses on advancing research and leadership in areas that align with The Commonwealth Fund’s key priorities, including:

  • Improving health insurance coverage
  • Expanding access to health care services
  • Enhancing quality of care
  • Increasing efficiency in health care delivery
  • Advancing health equity
  • Supporting evidence-based health policy reforms
  • Strengthening health care systems through innovative solutions

The fellowship aims to equip participants with the skills, knowledge, and international perspective needed to address complex health care challenges in their home countries and beyond.

Benefits of the Harkness Fellowship

Successful fellows gain access to numerous professional and academic opportunities, including:

1. International Research Experience

Participants conduct a high-impact policy-oriented research project with direct relevance to both the United States and their home country.

2. Access to Global Health Policy Experts

Fellows engage with leading policymakers, researchers, academics, clinicians, and health care executives across international health systems.

3. Leadership Development

The fellowship is designed to strengthen leadership capabilities and prepare participants for senior roles in health policy and health system transformation.

4. Professional Networking Opportunities

Participants become part of a distinguished global network of Harkness alumni, health policy experts, and decision-makers.

5. Policy Influence

Research projects are specifically designed to contribute to policy discussions and drive system-level improvements in health care delivery and governance.

6. Cross-Country Learning

The fellowship encourages comparative learning between the United States and participating countries, allowing fellows to identify transferable solutions and best practices.

Who Can Apply?

To be eligible for the 2027–2028 Harkness Fellowship, applicants must satisfy all of the following requirements:

Residency Requirement

Applicants must be established residents of one of the following countries:

  • Australia
  • Canada
  • France
  • Germany
  • The Netherlands
  • New Zealand
  • Norway
  • Singapore
  • United Kingdom

Applicants must also be eligible to receive a J-1 Research Scholar Visa and be committed to continuing their careers in the country from which they are applying.

Professional Experience

Candidates should be mid-career professionals with significant accomplishments and demonstrated leadership potential in health policy or health care practice.

Professionals may come from diverse sectors, including:

  • Government
  • Public policy
  • Health services research
  • Clinical practice
  • Health care management
  • Journalism
  • Health administration
  • Public health organizations
  • Academic institutions

Demonstrated Expertise

Applicants must possess:

  • Strong expertise in health policy issues
  • Evidence of influencing policy or practice
  • Experience conducting research, policy analysis, or health system leadership activities
  • A proven record of professional achievement

Research Project Requirement

Applicants must propose a research project that:

  • Addresses important health policy issues
  • Is relevant to both the United States and their home country
  • Focuses on policy or system-level change
  • Demonstrates feasibility and practical impact

Educational and Professional Qualifications

Candidates must have the educational background and professional experience necessary to successfully complete the proposed project and maximize the fellowship experience.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

The Commonwealth Fund strongly encourages applications from:

  • Historically marginalized communities
  • Underrepresented populations in health policy
  • Underrepresented groups in health care leadership
  • Professionals bringing diverse perspectives to health system reform

This commitment reflects the fellowship’s broader goal of promoting health equity and inclusive leadership across health care systems.

Application Components

Applicants must complete their submission through the Harkness Fellowship Portal and provide the following materials:

1. Statement of Professional Objectives

A detailed statement outlining career goals, leadership aspirations, and reasons for applying.

2. Curriculum Vitae (CV)

An updated professional resume highlighting qualifications, achievements, publications, and relevant experience.

3. Preliminary Project Proposal

Applicants must submit a policy-oriented project proposal of up to five pages (excluding references).

The proposal should:

  • Address a significant health policy issue
  • Demonstrate relevance to both countries
  • Outline methodology
  • Explain expected policy impact
  • Highlight opportunities for system-level change

4. Three Letters of Reference

Applicants must secure:

  • One Institutional or Organizational Reference from a supervisor or senior leader within their organization
  • Two Professional References from individuals who can assess their qualifications, achievements, and leadership potential

Applicants are advised to request references as early as possible to ensure timely submission.

5. Work Samples

Candidates must submit three examples of professional work, which may include:

  • Journal articles
  • Research papers
  • Policy reports
  • Technical reports
  • Published articles
  • Other professional writing samples

For unpublished materials, applicants should provide a brief explanation describing the work and its purpose.

Application Review and Selection Process

Applications undergo a rigorous review process conducted by Commonwealth Fund staff and expert review panels in each participating country.

Selection is based on:

  • Professional qualifications
  • Leadership potential
  • Commitment to improving health care
  • Quality of the proposed research project
  • Policy relevance of the research
  • Strength of recommendation letters
  • Potential to influence health policy and practice

Following initial review, shortlisted applicants may be invited to participate in interviews.

Interview Locations

Depending on the country of application, interviews may be conducted virtually or in person in:

  • Sydney (Australia)
  • Toronto (Canada)
  • Paris (France)
  • Berlin or The Hague (Germany and the Netherlands)
  • Auckland (New Zealand)
  • Oslo (Norway)
  • Singapore
  • London (United Kingdom)

Interview details will be communicated directly to shortlisted candidates.

Important Notes

Applicants should note that:

  • Fellowship awards cannot be used toward academic degree programs.
  • The fellowship cannot be undertaken simultaneously with another program.
  • Quantitative research applicants should carefully evaluate data availability and accessibility.
  • Qualitative research applicants should clearly explain their data collection methods, including interviews, focus groups, and document reviews.
  • Unsuccessful applicants are encouraged to reapply in future cycles.

Application Deadline

Application Opens: June 1, 2026

Application Deadline: November 1, 2026

Interested candidates should begin preparing their research proposals, reference letters, and supporting documents well in advance of the deadline.

How to Apply

Applicants must first create a Harkness Fellowship Portal account before beginning the application process.

After registration:

  1. Log in to the Harkness Fellowship Portal.
  2. Select “New User” if creating an account for the first time.
  3. Complete all required registration fields.
  4. Choose “Harkness Fellowship Applicant.”
  5. Complete and submit the online application before the deadline.

This fellowship presents an extraordinary opportunity for health policy professionals to contribute to meaningful health system reforms, develop international collaborations, and become part of a prestigious global network of leaders dedicated to improving health care delivery and health equity worldwide.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Commonwealth Fund Harkness Fellowships in Health Care Policy and Practice 2027–2028: Fully Funded Global Fellowship for Health Policy Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple International Internship Opportunities in Administration, Development Cooperation, Governance, Gender, Communications, and Sustainability
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Seoul Policy Centre is inviting applications from qualified university students, graduate students, and recent graduates for its internship opportunities. These internships provide aspiring professionals with a unique opportunity to gain practical experience within one of the world’s leading international development organizations while contributing to initiatives that support sustainable development […]

The post United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple International Internship Opportunities in Administration, Development Cooperation, Governance, Gender, Communications, and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Development Programme (UNDP) Seoul Policy Centre is inviting applications from qualified university students, graduate students, and recent graduates for its internship opportunities. These internships provide aspiring professionals with a unique opportunity to gain practical experience within one of the world’s leading international development organizations while contributing to initiatives that support sustainable development and global cooperation.

Hosted by the UNDP Seoul Policy Centre, these internship opportunities are offered twice annually and are designed to provide participants with meaningful exposure to international development work, policy implementation, governance, sustainability initiatives, communications, and partnership development.

Individuals interested in building careers in international development, public administration, governance, sustainability, communications, or partnership management are encouraged to explore these opportunities.

About the United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre

The UNDP Seoul Policy Centre serves as a strategic hub that supports development cooperation, knowledge exchange, and South-South and Triangular Cooperation. Through its work, the Centre promotes innovative development solutions and contributes to the achievement of the Sustainable Development Goals.

The Centre collaborates with governments, institutions, development partners, and stakeholders worldwide to identify and share effective development policies and practices. Interns at the Centre have the opportunity to work alongside experienced professionals and gain insights into global development processes.

Available Internship Positions

The current internship opportunities include:

1. Intern – Administration

This position provides exposure to the administrative operations that support the Centre’s day-to-day activities. Interns may contribute to office management processes, coordination activities, documentation, and operational support functions.

2. Intern – Development Cooperation

The Development Cooperation internship offers participants an opportunity to engage with initiatives that promote international development collaboration. Interns may support research, project coordination, knowledge-sharing activities, and development partnerships.

3. Intern – Governance and Gender (2 Positions)

The Governance and Gender internship focuses on advancing inclusive governance and gender equality initiatives. Selected interns may contribute to research, policy analysis, programme support, stakeholder engagement, and knowledge management activities.

4. Intern – Private Sector Partnerships

This internship provides practical experience in building and managing partnerships with private sector organizations. Interns may assist with partnership development, stakeholder mapping, communications, and collaboration initiatives that support sustainable development objectives.

5. Intern – Communications and Social Media

The Communications and Social Media internship is ideal for candidates interested in digital communications, content creation, public engagement, and storytelling. Interns may support communication campaigns, social media management, content development, and outreach activities.

6. Intern – Green Recovery and Transition (2 Positions)

The Green Recovery and Transition internship focuses on sustainability, environmental initiatives, and development strategies that support resilient and green economic growth. Interns may contribute to research, programme implementation support, and sustainability-focused projects.

Benefits of the UNDP Seoul Policy Centre Internship

Participating in a UNDP internship can provide numerous professional and personal development benefits, including:

  • Exposure to international development work.
  • Experience within a globally recognized United Nations agency.
  • Opportunities to work with experts in development policy and practice.
  • Enhanced understanding of sustainable development and global cooperation.
  • Development of professional skills in research, communication, administration, and project support.
  • Networking opportunities with international professionals and stakeholders.
  • Valuable experience that can strengthen future academic and career prospects.
  • Practical understanding of multilateral development institutions and programmes.

Why These Internships Stand Out

UNDP internships are highly sought after because they offer participants the chance to gain first-hand experience in addressing some of the world’s most pressing development challenges. Interns become part of an international environment that values innovation, collaboration, inclusiveness, and sustainable development.

The diversity of available internship tracks also allows applicants from various academic and professional backgrounds to identify opportunities aligned with their interests and career goals.

Recruitment Cycle

The UNDP Seoul Policy Centre offers internships twice every year:

  • Summer recruitment cycle
  • Winter recruitment cycle

Recruitment announcements are typically published through the official UNDP Seoul Policy Centre website and social media channels. Interested candidates are encouraged to monitor these platforms regularly for updates and new openings.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should submit their applications through the official application portal provided by the United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre.

Application Link:
https://www.undp.org/seoul/careers

Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the specific requirements and responsibilities associated with each internship position before submitting their applications.

Deadline

No specific application deadline was provided in the announcement.

Applications may be reviewed according to the recruitment cycle and vacancy timelines. Interested candidates are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible and regularly check the official website for updates regarding deadlines and position availability.

Final Thoughts

The United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internship Programme offers an excellent opportunity for university students, graduate students, and recent graduates seeking international exposure and professional development in the field of sustainable development.

With internship opportunities spanning administration, governance, development cooperation, communications, private sector partnerships, and green transition initiatives, applicants can gain valuable experience while contributing to meaningful global development efforts.

Prospective candidates should take advantage of this opportunity to develop practical skills, expand their professional networks, and gain insight into the work of one of the world’s leading development organizations.

APPLY HERE

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Internship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post United Nations Development Programme Seoul Policy Centre Internships 2026: Apply for Multiple International Internship Opportunities in Administration, Development Cooperation, Governance, Gender, Communications, and Sustainability appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
